items.title: Australian Prime Minister Apologizes For Saying He’d Have Sex With Kylie Minogue
items.summary: We last checked in on Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese last Halloween, shortly after he sparked some bad-faith outrage by wearing a Joy Division T-shirt. Now he's back in the news thanks to a more genuinely scandalous intersection with the music world. The post Australian Prime Minister Apologizes For Saying He’d Have Sex With Kylie Minogue appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Lockimara – “Every Day”
items.summary: Nicholas Gay, the musician who records swirly acoustic-electronic music under the name Lockimara, is currently living in New York, pursuing a master's degree in music production, after stints in Vancouver and Toronto.

items.title: Computerwife – “Nobody”
items.summary: Next week, Feeble Little Horse kick off their tour behind their really good new album bitknot. If you're going to see them, you should really show up early. For the first week of that tour, the opening act is Computerwife, the one-person alt-rock project from the Atlanta-born, New York-based musician Addie Warncke. Today, she releases her new song "Nobody." The post Computerwife – “Nobody” appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: James Ellis Ford (Simian Mobile Disco) announces new album, shares “Overtones”
items.summary: ’Lost in Another World’ was made over two weeks in early 2025 while in the hospital between rounds of chemotherapy following a diagnosis of myeloid leukemia. It’s out August 14.

items.title: Danny Elfman preparing new album, announces fall tour
items.summary: He also has festival dates, including Shaky Knees, Louder Than Life, and Aftershock.

items.title: Panda Bear & Sonic Boom tell us why their album won’t be on streaming services + yodeling, police sirens & more (BV Interviews)
items.summary: Panda Bear & Sonic Boom’s second album, ‘A ? of When,’ is out July 10 and you can listen to our full conversation on the latest episode of the BV Interviews podcast.

items.title: Violet Grohl Finds Her Own Frequency
items.summary: The two-building compound tucked into an industrial stretch northwest of Los Angeles looks nondescript from the outside, but inside it’s a rock’n’roll nerd’s dream come true. Rooms are full of gear stacked to the ceilings and giant road cases stenciled with the name of one of the world’s biggest rock bands make hallway navigation a […]

items.title: Kelsy Karter is Obsessed with Obsession
items.summary: In a recording studio deep in the San Fernando Valley, Kelsy Karter is singing a new song about obsession. The tune is called “Softer,” and, like much of her work, its lyrics are raw and autobiographical. This time, they draw on her recent diagnosis with a form of Obsessive-compulsive disorder known as “Pure O.” Yet […]

items.title: The Passions of John Darnielle
items.summary: “I can write a song under fire,” says John Darnielle, from his home in North Carolina. “I don’t sit around waiting for lightning to strike me before I write. I write when I write as a matter of course. The whole process is a pleasure.” The musician/writer has been releasing albums at a steady clip […]
